leadText: Aura Sphere (Japanese: はどうだん Wave Bomb) is a damage-dealing Fighting-type move introduced in Generation IV. It was TR56 in Pokémon Sword and Shield, and is TM112 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generations IV and V 1.2 Generation VI onward 1.3 Generation VIII 1.3.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.4 Generation IX 1.4.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generations IV and V [ edit | edit source ] Aura Sphere inflicts damage and bypasses accuracy checks to always hit, unless the target is in the semi-invulnerable turn of a move such as Dig or Fly . It can hit non-adjacent targets in Triple Battles . Generation VI onward [ edit | edit source ] Aura Sphere's power was lowered from 90 to 80. Additionally, its power is boosted by 50% if the user has Mega Launcher , and Pokémon with Bulletproof are immune to it. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Aura Sphere's PP has been reduced to 10. sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description D P Pt HG SS PBR The user looses a blast of aura power from deep within its body. This move is certain to hit. B W B2 W2 The user looses a blast of aura power from deep within its body at the target. This move is certain to hit. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA The user lets loose a blast of aura power from deep within its body at the target. This attack never misses. S V The user lets loose a pulse of aura power from deep within its body at the target. This attack never misses. ZA The user attacks by letting loose a pulse of aura power from within its body.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD T D S Inflicts damage on the target, even at a distance. It never misses. B S L はなれたポケモンに ダメージをあたえる かならず めいちゅうする Conq. The user looses a blast of aura power from deep within its body at the target. This move is certain to hit. MD GtI It damages even a faraway Pokémon. It never misses. Super Smash Bros. for Nintendo 3DS/Wii U Charge a ball of energy to use on your foes. * S MD MD RT DX It damages a Pokémon, even one far away. It never misses.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Aura Sphere bears resemblances both in visuals and in its Japanese name (はどうだん Hadōdan ) to the Hadouken attack in the Street Fighter video game series. In Pokémon X and Y , a camera clipping glitch occurs when the player uses Aura Sphere against some of Drasna 's Pokémon. By GlitchyGarchomp This video is not available on Bulbapedia; instead, you can watch the video on YouTube here .
